commit.message:
Fix/hovmester verify merge queue (#249)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix example generation: use value instead of pointer for model results in response envelopes (#1912)

* fix: example generation uses value instead of pointer for model results in response envelopes

The byValue parameter was hardcoded to false when generating example
response bodies for non-pageable methods, causing model results to be
rendered with & (pointer) syntax. However, modelResult and
polymorphicResult types are anonymously embedded by value in response
structs. The pageable case already correctly used byValue=true.

Fix: compute resultByValue based on the result kind - only
monomorphicResult uses its own byValue property; all other result
types default to by value.

commit.message:
fix: backspace at start of continuation line rejoins previous line

When in multiline input mode (Shift+Enter/Ctrl+J), pressing backspace
at the start of an empty continuation line now pops the previous line
back from the continuation buffer, allowing the user to edit it.

- Intercepts DEL (0x7f) and BS (0x08) in raw stdin handler
- Checks cursor position and line emptiness via readline internals
- Updates prompt prefix when transitioning out of continuation mode

commit.message:
fix(verify): add merge_group trigger for merge queue compatibility (#248)

## Problem

`pull_request_target` does not fire in merge queue context, causing
`verify-hovmester-sync` to show as **Expected — Waiting for status to be
reported** and blocking auto-merge of hovmester sync PRs.

## Fix

Add `merge_group` trigger to `hovmester-verify.yml`. When the PR enters
the merge queue:
- Verification already happened on `pull_request_target` (scope check +
auto-approve)
- `merge_group` event fires → job condition doesn't match → job skips →
GitHub reports neutral/success
- Merge queue gets the green light

The `if` condition is updated to: `github.event_name == 'merge_group' ||
(existing conditions)`

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(jetson): pre-build engines on startup + simplify config flow

- SyncEngine.run(): check desired model_versions vs loaded engines
  at startup, trigger builds immediately instead of waiting for
  first poll cycle (5min). Engines built in background thread.
- main.py: simplified startup config to single source (SyncEngine)
  instead of duplicate device_state read. Removed redundant block.

Config flow: Admin → Supabase → SyncEngine (single writer) →
  main.py reads via get_active_config()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Semgrep SAST security scanning (#518)

* Add Semgrep SAST security scanning

Add a non-blocking Semgrep SAST workflow that runs on pull requests and
pushes to main. Uses the p/security-audit ruleset with SHA-pinned actions
for supply-chain safety. Configured with continue-on-error so it
complements existing dependency protections without blocking merges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Semgrep to use latest Docker image instead of deprecated action

The semgrep/semgrep-action@v1 is deprecated and runs Semgrep 1.36.0
(unsupported since v1.76.0). Switch to using the returntocorp/semgrep
Docker container directly, which always pulls the latest version.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve Semgrep: pin version, add SARIF upload, PR comments, timeout

- Pin semgrep/semgrep:1.157.0 Docker image (was unpinned latest)
- Move continue-on-error from job level to step level
- Add SARIF upload to GitHub Security tab (continue-on-error for non-GHAS repos)
- Add PR comment step summarizing findings
- Add timeout-minutes: 15
- Add security-events: write and pull-requests: write permissions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SARIF upload: add actions:read permission, upgrade to codeql-action v4

- Add actions: read permission (fixes 'Resource not accessible by integration' error)
- Upgrade github/codeql-action from v3 to v4 (v3 deprecated Dec 2026)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: worktree-aware session discovery

CLI now uses git rev-parse --show-toplevel to find .groundcrew/
root, then walks up directories as fallback. Works from
subdirectories and git worktrees.

Hooks now use git rev-parse for consistent root detection.
Banner updated: Shift+Enter instead of Ctrl+J.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add v14→v15 migration for users who ran browser-companion build

The browser-companion feature branch used migrations v12→v13 and
v13→v14 for its own tables, bumping the DB to user_version=14.
Users who ran that build have a DB at v14, so the existing
v12→v13 OneDrive migration never fires.

This adds a parallel v14→v15 migration block with identical OneDrive
table definitions (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) for those users.
Fresh installs are now set to user_version=15.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: orion-ui
repository.description: Orion is a retro-styled, cross-platform UI framework written in C. The framework is not embedded or mobile-targeted — it is a desktop GUI framework designed for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Its central design philosophy is to faithfully reproduce the Windows API (Win32) message-based programming model in a portable, hardware-accelerated context.

commit.message:
fix(jetson): find trtexec in known paths when not in PATH

trtexec on Jetson is typically at /usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec
which is not in systemd service PATH. Now searches known locations
before falling back to error.

fix: add DB migration v12→v13 for OneDrive tables

Existing databases at user_version=12 missed the onedrive_roots,
onedrive_customer_folders, and onedrive_files tables since they were
only added to the fresh-install schema (getSchema). This migration
applies those tables to all existing databases on next startup.
Also bumps the fresh-install user_version to 13.

- Block YAML serialization/deserialization

commit.message:
feat: native POCO serializer and deserializer

YamlSerializer — public API:
- Serialize<T>/Deserialize<T> with YamlSerializerOptions
- DeserializeAll for multi-document streams
- UTF-8 byte overloads

YamlObjectMapper — reflection-based deserializer:
- Type-cached property discovery
- [YamlPropertyName]/[YamlIgnore] attributes
- Scalar→CLR conversion (string/bool/int/long/float/double/decimal/DateTime/Guid/enum)
- Collection mapping (T[], List<T>, Dictionary<K,V>)
- YamlNamingPolicy (PascalCase/camelCase/snake_case/kebab-case)
- YAML 1.1 legacy boolean support

YamlObjectWriter — direct POCO→YAML serializer:
- Block-style output with configurable indentation
- Multiline strings as literal block scalars (|)
- Smart quoting for special values

commit.message:
🗃️ feat: semantic intent filter for orientation-specific queries 🗃️

Add QueryIntent detection and ResultMatchesIntent filter so that
orientation-specific queries (e.g. "lesbian", "girl on girl", "sapphic")
reject results whose titles clearly indicate biological male participation.
Changes:
- taxonomy.go: add QueryIntent struct, DetectQueryIntent(), and
ResultMatchesIntent() with conservative whole-word matching
- femaleOnlyTerms: triggers female-only mode for 12 orientation terms
- malePresenceWords: 14 explicit-male tokens (cock/dick/bbc/stepbro/
facial/cumshot/handjob/etc); strapon/dildo intentionally excluded
- containsWholeWord(): safe word-boundary checker preventing
false positives like "german" matching "man"
- DetectQueryIntent(): also populates HasAgeTypes (teen/milf/mature)
for future cross-type validation
- manager.go: detect query intent once before result collection loop;
apply ResultMatchesIntent after existing term-match filter
Conservative design principles:
- Only filter when contradiction is unambiguous in the title
- "strapon", "dildo", "strap-on" are NOT male indicators
- Word-boundary checks prevent substring false positives
- Has no effect on non-orientation queries (zero overhead)
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: character-level YAML 1.2 scanner

Utf8YamlScanner implements a libyaml-inspired character-level tokenizer:
- Simple-key tracking with indent stack
- Flow/block context separation
- Block scalar scanning (literal |, folded >)
- Double/single quoted scalar scanning with escape sequences
- Plain scalar scanning with document indicator detection
- Anchor, alias, and tag token recognition
- AggressiveInlining on hot-path methods
- Pooled StringBuilders for zero per-scalar allocation

commit.message:
fix: add Ctrl+J as universal newline key for multiline input

Most macOS terminals (Terminal.app, iTerm2) send identical \r for both
Enter and Shift+Enter — there's no way to distinguish them. Only Kitty
and WezTerm support the Kitty keyboard protocol (\x1b[13;2u).

Fix: Add Ctrl+J (\n / LF) as cross-terminal newline. In raw mode, Enter
sends \r (CR) while Ctrl+J sends \n (LF), making them distinguishable
on ALL terminal emulators.

Newline options:
- Ctrl+J: Works everywhere (recommended)
- Shift+Enter: Works on Kitty/WezTerm (Kitty protocol)
- Alt+Enter: Works on iTerm2 and some terminals
- Backslash + Enter: Always works (manual continuation)

Also: added multiline hint to banner UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix security issues: CSRF protection, secure cookie, auth rate limiting

- Set session cookie secure flag to true in production, add httpOnly and sameSite
- Add session-based CSRF middleware (middleware/csrf.js) applied after session/flash
- Apply authLimiter directly to POST /login and POST /register handlers in routes/auth.js
- Add _csrf hidden fields to all POST forms across all views

security: add rate limiting, reduce upload limit, warn on missing session secret

- Add express-rate-limit: 20 req/15min on auth routes, 120 req/min on all others
- Reduce multer file upload limit from 50MB to 20MB
- Warn at startup if SESSION_SECRET env variable is not set
- Use SESSION_SECRET constant to avoid duplicate fallback strings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: vortexsupportnow-max <271516904+vortexsupportnow-max@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: bump CLI version to 0.15.11

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip update_playing_status when duration is unknown (0ms)

Ynison rejects progress updates where progress > duration with error
400030001 and disconnects the WebSocket.  When a track just changed,
duration may still be 0 (stream not yet probed, Ynison state not yet
updated).  Skip sending progress until duration is known.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ma-provider-yandex-ynison
repository.description: Yandex Music Connect (Ynison) plugin for Music Assistant

commit.message:
feat: bracketed paste, left-arrow fix, display results between tasks

CLI improvements:
- Add bracketed paste mode — multiline paste is buffered and submitted
  as single task instead of auto-submitting after first line
- Fix left-arrow bug — clearGhost() no longer erases typed text when
  cursor is mid-line (only clears ghost when ghostLen > 0)
- Add Alt+Enter as universal newline fallback (works in all terminals)
- Enable Kitty keyboard protocol for Shift+Enter newlines
- Disable bracketed paste + Kitty protocol on exit

Server improvements:
- Display task results between tasks — DISPLAY_TO_USER field in mark_done
- Add display instruction to TASK LOOP PROTOCOL and get_task description
- Soften CRITICAL_NEXT_ACTION to allow brief result display

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix image loading fallback for common path typos (De bse -> De base, missing 1- prefix)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add complete FSI Championship web application

- Node.js + Express.js backend with EJS templates
- better-sqlite3 synchronous SQLite database
- Dark racing theme CSS with Italian flag accents
- Authentication: login, register, session management
- Drivers: profiles, stats, race history
- Races: calendar, results entry, detailed results grid
- Standings: championship table with per-race points
- Penalties: forum-style report system with admin review
- Admin dashboard: user management, seasons, quick links
- Points system: 25-18-15-12-10-8-6-4-2-1
- Responsive design with mobile hamburger menu
- Seed data: admin user (admin/admin123) + FSI Season 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: convergio-orchestrator
repository.description: Orchestrator — plans, tasks, waves, Thor gate, reaper, workspace management

commit.message:
feat: animations controlled exclusively by Animation Studio

- Sidebar: remove per-object 'Animate' toggle, 'Creation Animation' accordion
  and 'Animate All' global toggle — Studio is now the sole animation control
- InteractiveSvgCanvas: remove animAngle RAF loop and globalAnimating-based
  rotation; objects render rotation from obj.rotation set by Studio keyframes
- InteractiveCanvas: remove animAngleRef auto-spin; remove angle param from
  drawObject; canvas RAF loop already runs every frame so Studio updates
  applied via updateObject() are picked up instantly
- All 33 unit tests still pass, build clean

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(engagement): story status summary service (#137)

TASK-P3-08: Pure SQL-based story status service for engagement dashboard.

- Factory createStoryStatusService(deps) with 3 prepared statements
- Chapter progress with ROW_NUMBER() 1-based dense rank
- Content-edit recency via document_versions allowlist (8 content reasons)
- Active foreshadowing from KG entities (json_extract resolved check)
- L0 memory with project-scope precedence
- 30s per-project cache with invalidation
- 35 tests across 9 sections

Closes #136

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add immutable username field with tenant-prefixed Keycloak username (#21)

- Add Username property to ApplicationUser model + EF migration
- Add Username to CreateUserRequest DTO (optional, defaults to email local part)
- Add Username to UserResponse and UserWithTenantsResponse DTOs
- Username is immutable: editable only during creation, read-only on edit
- Keycloak username format: {tenantId}.{username} for cross-tenant uniqueness
- DB stores base username (without tenant prefix)
- Update KeycloakAdminService.CreateUserAsync and CreateUserWithoutPasswordAsync
- Add username field to UserManagement.razor with auto-derive from email
- Add username field to EmployeeManagement.razor inline user creation
- Update EmployeeUserEndpoints to pass username on employee+user creation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
